title: /kernel
summary_text: Yes, technically. `scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh` accepts `mode` as a free-form argument and writes it straight into the JSON receipt with no allowlist or validation, so `blocked` would be stored and later reported by `status` just like any other string ([`scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh`](./scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh#L49-L99)). The mismatch is semantic, not technical. The `/kernel` prompt defines only `normal` and `degraded-allowed` as valid shapes, and says `BLOCKED` is only valid when neither shape can be established ([`.codex/prompts/kernel.md`](./.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L61-L66)). `CODEX.md` reinforces that the bootstrap receipt should carry live evidence and that the first valid acknowledgement only exists for an actually active lane set, not a blocked bootstrap ([`CODEX.md`](./CODEX.md#L154-L163)).
